titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender, 2 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Sir Wilfred Bliss Spender (1876 - 1960) to his wife, Lady Lillian Spender (1880-1968). Wilfrid expresses his pride and sadness at the men of Ulster 'sacrificing their lives by the thousands' with no apparent result, due to the failure of the (non-Ulster) divisions on their right and left. He characterises this doing of 'the most splendid things with no results' as the usual fate of the Ulsterman. This letter is part of a series of correspondence from Sir Wilfrid Bliss Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War.Sir Wilfrid Spender was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender remained in contact with the Ulster Unionist leadership, and consistently opposed Home Rule and the six-county solution.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
July /16 july 1916 My pr I am a proud but sadâ
dend man. I have seen our
attack as no other man
in this world could Do. I believe
I have seen them win through
an inferno which other
troops failing ours
got through. I have seen
their advance as if in
drill on parade winning the
admiration of the next corps
staff & of all who saw them . I know our men
are sacrificing their lives by
the thousand. I know that
their cause is probably in vain owing
to the failure on our right & left.
It seems to be the fate of the Ulsterman
to do the most splendid things with
no results. I am not going in to
the question of our losses which run very
heavy. I know you will do all you can. Don't spurn any experts who'll
save you for your work a little in your work.
Probly Capt Bruce's sister may come
& help. I don't intend to write
of what I saw,but you can tell
Sredward that I think half our
men deserve VCs & Crosses but
will not get them of course. Always your bw
Wilfred Spender .
